[Last 3 Agent Mail messages from relevant threads]

## Key Decisions Made
[From CM procedural memory for this project]

- [x] Completed: [completed beads]
- [ ] In progress: [in_progress beads]
- [ ] Blocked by: [blocking issues]

Reread AGENTS.md and continue from where you left off.

## Scope
- Format RecoveryContext into markdown prompt
- Apply token limit (config: max_recovery_tokens = 2000)
- Prioritize most relevant information if truncating
- Send to agent pane on spawn

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery prompt readable and actionable
- Respects token limit configuration
- Graceful when partial context available
- Sent before user's first prompt

## Technical Notes

 {"id":"bd-n770","title":"Add YAML swarm config file - define entire swarm (agents, prompts, assignments) in one declarative file","description":"## Overview
Define entire swarm configuration in one declarative YAML file.

## Config Format (swarm.yaml)
```yaml
name: myproject-swarm
session: myproject

agents:
  claude: 10
  codex: 10  
  gemini: 4
  
options:
  no-user: true
  auto-assign: true
  
prompts:
  init: |
    Read AGENTS.md and register with Agent Mail.
    Check your inbox and respond to any messages.
    Use bv --robot-triage to find work.
  review: |
    Review code written by fellow agents...
  post-compaction: |
    Reread AGENTS.md. Use ultrathink.

assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 24
  template: impl
  round-robin: false
  
monitor:
  stuck_threshold_seconds: 300
  auto_restart_stuck: true
  completion_patterns:
    - 'bead complete'
    - 'task finished'
```

## Commands
```bash
ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml           # Start full swarm
ntm spawn --config=swarm.yaml           # Just spawn from config
ntm assign --config=swarm.yaml          # Use assignment settings
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses YAML config format
- [ ] Validates required fields
- [ ] Supports all spawn options
- [ ] Supports prompt templates
- [ ] Supports assignment settings
- [ ] Supports monitor settings
- [ ] Clear error messages for invalid config

## Tests Required
- Unit test: YAML parsing and validation
- Unit test: Config defaults
- E2E test: Full swarm from config file","notes":"Closed as dropped: avoid new YAML format; reuse existing pipeline/recipe configs if declarative setup is needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:47.613482061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:34.562867377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:17.646542265Z","close_reason":"Dropped per canonical assign flow; redundant/overlapping entry points","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n770","depends_on_id":"bd-2rqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-n9bcu","title":"Feature: Prompt Engine - Mode-specific preambles and injection","description":"# Feature: Prompt Engine - Mode-specific preambles with schema enforcement

Each mode agent receives a structured prompt with three mandatory components:
1. Base instructions (tone, structure, constraints)
2. Mode preamble (the reasoning 'lens')
3. **Output schema contract** (mandatory structured output)

## Prompt Structure

### 1. Base Instructions
```
You are participating in a reasoning ensemble analyzing: {problem_statement}

Context Pack:
{context_pack}

Your role: Apply your assigned reasoning mode rigorously.

Constraints:
- Stay within your reasoning mode's strengths
- Be explicit about confidence levels (numeric or high|medium|low)
- Reference specific evidence (file:line)
- Flag your mode's known failure modes
- DO NOT exceed {token_cap} tokens
```

### 2. Mode Preamble (The Lens)
```
## Your Reasoning Mode: {mode_name}

{mode_description}

What you excel at:
{best_for}

How you differ from other modes:
{differentiator}

Common failure modes to avoid:
{failure_modes}
```

### 3. Output Schema Contract (MANDATORY)
```
## REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT

You MUST structure your response as valid YAML with these exact fields:

mode_id: {mode_id}           # optional; system will inject if missing
thesis: |
  Your one-sentence main insight
confidence: 0.0-1.0           # numeric or high|medium|low

top_findings:
  - finding: Description of finding
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    confidence: 0.0-1.0        # numeric or high|medium|low
    evidence_pointer: path/to/file.go:142
    reasoning: How you reached this

risks:
  - risk: Description of risk
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    likelihood: 0.0-1.0        # numeric or high|medium|low
    mitigation: Suggested mitigation
    affected_areas: [string]

recommendations:
  - recommendation: What to do
    priority: critical|high|medium|low
    effort: low|medium|high
    rationale: Why this helps
    related_findings: [int]    # indices into top_findings (0-based)

questions_for_user:
  - question: What needs clarification?
    context: Why this matters
    blocking: false
    suggested_answers: [string]

failure_modes_to_watch:
  - mode: Potential failure of my analysis
    description: What could go wrong
    indicators: [string]
    prevention: How to guard against it

NON-COMPLIANT OUTPUTS WILL BE REJECTED.
```

## Schema Validation

PromptInjector validates all outputs:
- Parse YAML structure
- Check required fields present
- Validate enum values (impact/priority/etc.)
- Accept numeric or qualitative likelihood/confidence values
- Ensure evidence_pointers are valid file references
- Validate list fields when present (affected_areas, related_findings, suggested_answers, indicators)
- Inject mode_id if missing (warn)
- Flag non-compliant outputs for human review

## Testing Coverage
- Unit: bd-xkrnc (preamble rendering + schema contract fields)
- Unit: bd-qeoex (schema validator exhaustive cases)
- E2E: bd-18ds7 (full pipeline includes preamble injection + schema validation)

## Files
- internal/ensemble/preamble.go - Template structure
- internal/ensemble/preambles/ - Mode-specific content
- internal/ensemble/schema_validator.go - Output validation
- internal/swarm/prompt_injector.go - Injection logic

## Logging Requirements
- Log prompt assembly start with mode_id, mode_code, pane, agent_type, token_cap
- Log schema contract version/hash and missing required fields
- Log validation summary (valid/invalid counts, first error per mode)
- Log injection outcome per pane (success/failure + error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompt assembly deterministic for same inputs (stable ordering)
- [ ] Output schema contract matches OutputSchema types (no drift)
- [ ] Schema validation rejects non-compliant outputs and reports errors
- [ ] Logging captures per-mode injection and validation status","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:25:44.569622243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:44:13.143101802Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:44:13.142546325Z","close_reason":"Feature complete - Prompt Engine with mode-specific preambles and schema enforcement:

## Implementation Summary
All components verified complete:
1. **preamble.go** - PreambleEngine with base instructions template and schema contract
2. **injector.go** - EnsembleInjector wrapping BasicInjector for mode-aware injection
3. **schema_validator.go** - Full validation of ModeOutput with ParseYAML/Normalize/Validate
4. **types.go** - OutputSchema types (ModeOutput, Finding, Risk, Recommendation, Question, FailureModeWarning)

## Acceptance Criteria Verified
- [x] Prompt assembly deterministic (Go templates with stable ordering)
- [x] Schema contract matches OutputSchema types (all fields aligned)
- [x] Schema validation rejects non-compliant outputs with detailed errors
- [x] Logging captures mode_id, mode_code, pane, agent_type, success/failure

## Key Features
- Three-section preamble: base instructions + mode section + schema contract
- Context pack rendering with project brief and user context
- Mode metadata auto-generation from ReasoningMode fields
- SchemaVersion tracking (1.0.0)
- Support for numeric or qualitative confidence/likelihood values","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-1hlhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-2pl9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-4io74","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-qeoex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-wx2qv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-xkrnc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-nxh7p","title":"Feature: XF robot bridge for archive search","description":"## Overview
Provide a minimal wrapper around XF to search X/Twitter archives in robot workflows.

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-xf-search=QUERY [--limit=N] [--since=DATE]
```

**Naming Pattern**: Follows tool bridge convention `--robot-<tool>-<action>=PARAM`

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "query": "search terms",
  "count": 5,
  "hits": [
    {
      "url": "https://twitter.com/...",
      "text": "Tweet content...",
      "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      "author": "handle"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Handles missing xf gracefully with actionable error
- JSON output is stable and schema-documented
- Supports optional limit and date filtering","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-pqwk","title":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation","description":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation.

## Problem Being Solved

Currently you need to type full session name like `vnt destructive_command_guard`. Should support prefix matching.

## Behavior

- If prefix matches exactly one session: use it
- If prefix matches multiple sessions: show matching options, ask user to be more specific
- If prefix matches zero sessions: show error with available sessions

## Examples

```bash
# Currently required
ntm view destructive_command_guard

# Desired
ntm view destr  # works if only one session starts with 'destr'
vnt destr       # same with alias

# Ambiguous
ntm view my
# Error: 'my' matches multiple sessions: my_project, my_test
# Please be more specific.
```

## Implementation

Update session name resolution in all commands:
- ntm view SESSION
- ntm kill SESSION
- ntm send SESSION
- ntm health SESSION
- etc.

## Robot Mode API
Robot mode requires exact names (no fuzzy matching).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Prefix matching works for all commands taking SESSION arg
2. Exact match preferred over prefix match
3. Multiple matches show list and error
4. Zero matches show available sessions
5. Robot mode requires exact names
6. Works with vnt alias

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/session/resolve_test.go
- Test exact match priority
- Test unique prefix resolution
- Test ambiguous prefix error

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PREFIX] Session Prefix Resolution Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PREFIX]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing prefix session names"
ntm spawn test_session_one --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm spawn test_session_two --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm view test_session_o && echo "Prefix worked"
ntm kill test_session_one --force
ntm kill test_session_two --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Prefix test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partial session names work for disambiguation
2. Prefix matching finds unique session
3. Multiple matches returns error with suggestions
4. Works with all session-accepting commands
5. Unit tests for matching logic
6. E2E test with multiple sessions","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-ptu2","title":"Task: Add event markers and details overlay","description":"## Overview
Add discrete event markers on the timeline with hover/select details.

## Deliverables
1. Event marker types:
   - Prompt sent (▶): When user sends prompt to agent
   - Completion (✓): When agent finishes a task
   - Error (✗): When error detected
   - Start/Stop (◆): Session lifecycle events

2. Marker rendering:
   - Small icons above timeline bar at event time
   - Different colors for different event types
   - Clustered markers when events too close together

3. Details overlay:
   - Show popup when marker selected
   - Include: timestamp, event type, details
   - For prompts: first 50 chars of message
   - For errors: error message

4. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to navigate between markers
   - Enter to expand marker details
   - Esc to close overlay

## Implementation Notes
- Use unicode symbols for markers
- Popup positioning to avoid edge overflow
- Cache marker positions for efficient navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Markers appear at correct times
- Different event types visually distinct
- Details overlay shows on selection
- Navigation between markers works
- Unit tests for marker placement","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.496917374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:20:48.617338102Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T04:20:48.617205812Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-3h0n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qjsu","title":"E2E Tests: File reservation with real Agent Mail","description":"## E2E Test: ntm + Agent Mail File Reservation Integration

**Note:** File reservation is implemented in MCP Agent Mail. This tests ntm's integration with that existing system.

### Prerequisites
- MCP Agent Mail server running (`am` health check passes)
- Test workspace with beads

### Test Scenarios

1. **Basic reservation flow**
   - Spawn agent via ntm
   - Assign bead with file paths
   - Verify Agent Mail shows reservation for agent
   - Complete bead
   - Verify reservation released

2. **Conflict detection**
   - Spawn 2 agents
   - Agent 1 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Agent 2 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Verify ntm reports conflict from Agent Mail
   - Verify holder info shown

3. **Reservation on assign**
   - Run ntm assign with --auto
   - Verify file_reservation_paths called for each assignment
   - Check Agent Mail state reflects reservations

### Verification Commands
```bash

 {"id":"bd-qn9gc","title":"Task: Implement mode loader with embedded/user/project sources in internal/ensemble/loader.go","description":"# Task: Implement mode loader with embedded/user/project sources in internal/ensemble/loader.go

## Purpose
Load and merge modes from multiple sources with proper precedence: embedded < user global < project local.

## Implementation
Use the existing ModeCatalog struct from internal/ensemble/types.go.

```go
// internal/ensemble/loader.go
func LoadModeCatalog() (*ModeCatalog, error) {
    // 1. Start with embedded modes (all 80)
    // 2. Merge user modes from ~/.config/ntm/modes.toml
    // 3. Merge project modes from .ntm/modes.toml
    // Later sources override earlier (project > user > embedded)
}

func (c *ModeCatalog) GetMode(id string) (*ReasoningMode, bool)
func (c *ModeCatalog) GetModeByCode(code string) (*ReasoningMode, bool)
func (c *ModeCatalog) ListModes() []ReasoningMode
func (c *ModeCatalog) ListByCategory(cat ModeCategory) []ReasoningMode
func (c *ModeCatalog) ListByTier(tier ModeTier) []ReasoningMode
```

## TOML Format for User/Project Modes
```toml
[[modes]]
id = "my-custom-mode"
code = "L1"                # optional, must be A–L taxonomy
name = "My Custom Mode"
category = "Domain"
tier = "advanced"          # core|advanced|experimental
short_desc = "A custom reasoning mode"
description = "..."
outputs = "..."
best_for = ["use-case-1", "use-case-2"]
failure_modes = ["pitfall-1", "pitfall-2"]
differentiator = "..."
icon = "🎯"
color = "#FF6B6B"
preamble_key = "my-custom-mode"
```

## Defaults
- If `tier` omitted: default to `advanced` for user/project modes (explicit opt-in required in UI).
- Embedded catalog marks curated modes as `core` and the rest as `advanced`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Loads embedded modes at init time
- [ ] Merges user and project modes with precedence
- [ ] Validates required fields, category, code, tier
- [ ] Thread-safe access
- [ ] Graceful handling of missing/invalid files","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:15.314750227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:04:24.129353392Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T23:04:24.129329687Z","close_reason":"Implemented ModeLoader with embedded < user < project precedence.
